mission.title: envoy: [bug] Client x-request-id Mangled by RequestIdExtension::setTraceStatus
mission.description: [bug] Client x-request-id Mangled by RequestIdExtension::setTraceStatus. This issue affects 13+ users.

steps.title: Identify envoy troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your envoy deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy -n envoy --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
When providing an x-request-id header in the request (rather than have it injected by Envoy), a UUID has its 14th byte modified. This results in a different UUID than provided by the caller, breaking the x-request-id passed along a call chain.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [bug] Client x-request-id Mangled by…
steps.description:
For everyone watching this issue I'm going to fix this, but I'm trying to sort through some details. First, the request ID header mangling is behind this guard AFAICT:
```yaml
This code doesn't distinguish either:
- UUID vs non-UUID string (of length 36)
- UUID generated internally vs provided in original request

Illustrative flow:
- Request created by client, with 'x-request-id' header containing value 'aaaaaaaa-bbbb-4ccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee'
- Request is received by Envoy, which sets Tracing Status on the 14th byte
- Request now has 'x-request-id' header containing value 'aaaaaaaa-bbbb-9ccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee' (or 'bccc', 'eccc')

### Ask
Ideally, setTraceStatus() should only update trace status on valid UUIDs generated by Envoy itself, and should not update values 
```

steps.title: Confirm [bug] Client x-request-id Mangled by… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy -n envoy --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: For everyone watching this issue I'm going to fix this, but I'm trying to sort through some details.
codeSnippets:
This code doesn't distinguish either:
- UUID vs non-UUID string (of length 36)
- UUID generated internally vs provided in original request

Illustrative flow:
- Request created by client, with 'x-request-id' header containing value 'aaaaaaaa-bbbb-4ccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee'
- Request is received by Envoy, which sets Tracing Status on the 14th byte
- Request now has 'x-request-id' header containing value 'aaaaaaaa-bbbb-9ccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee' (or 'bccc', 'eccc')

### Ask
Ideally, setTraceStatus() should only update trace status on valid UUIDs generated by Envoy itself, and should not update values provided/retained from caller.

### Config
Seems to occur either naturally (when request is deemed to be x-request-internal) or when using the 'preserve_external_request_id' connection manager flag.
**Example - Request populated 'x-request-id, UUID with 14th byte == 4 (no trace)**

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
